response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with strong full-stack and some systems-level background, but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. He lacks native-level Japanese (a hard requirement), has no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and his C/UNIX exposure is limited to a short stint at National Instruments over a decade ago. His career centers on web platforms in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, or security technologies like MACsec and 802.1X.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/hardware contexts
Low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services) shows systems-programming aptitude
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and teaching/mentoring experience
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a mandatory requirement
Minimal recent C programming and no Linux kernel analysis or modification experience
No background in network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 switch development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or kernel experience
